etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 皮(皮) (pí, "surface") -> wave
etymology.interpretation: The character 波(bō, "wave") combines 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") as the semantic component, indicating its association with bodies of water, and 皮(皮) (pí, "surface") as the phonetic component. The image evokes the concept of water moving across the surface, characteristic of a wave.

examples.chinese: 海浪卷起了大波(海浪卷起了大波)。
examples.english: The ocean waves rolled into big waves.

examples.chinese: 湖面上泛起了细小的波纹(湖面上泛起了細小的波紋)。
examples.pinyin: Húmiàn shàng fàn qǐle xì xiǎo de bōwén.
examples.english: Small ripples appeared on the surface of the lake.

examples.chinese: 心如止水，波澜不惊(心如止水，波瀾不驚)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn rú zhǐshuǐ, bōlán bù jīng.
examples.english: The heart is like still water, unruffled by waves.
